title: Pediatric abdominal pain follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.
assessment.rationale: Supports paediatric pain, systemic, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent and psychosocial history for child abdominal-pain follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.

assessment.text: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.
assessment.rationale: Supports appearance, hydration and abdominal examination for child abdominal-pain follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute

source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

assessment.text: Lists severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting, suspected surgical disease, admission, follow-up, and parent advice contexts.
assessment.rationale: Supports severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting and clinician-led escalation context for child abdominal-pain follow-up; no decision is generated.
